q: In which country were the ancient Olympic Games born?
exp: They were held at Olympia, Greece, from 776 BC.

q: How many players are in a rugby union team?
exp: There is also rugby sevens, Olympic since 2016.

q: What is the distance of a marathon?
exp: The distance recalls the run from Marathon to Athens.

q: Which swimmer won 23 Olympic gold medals?
exp: He is the most decorated Olympian of all time.

q: Which country has won the most football World Cups?
exp: Brazil has won the World Cup five times.

q: How many sets must a man win at a Grand Slam tennis match?
exp: Men's Grand Slam matches are best of 5 sets.

q: In which sport are there 'strikes' and 'spares'?
exp: A 'strike' is knocking down all 10 pins on the first roll.

q: Which sport combines swimming, cycling and running?
exp: The order is swim, then bike, then run.

q: How many points is a try worth in rugby union?
exp: After a try (5 points) there is also the conversion (2 points).

q: Which cyclist is famous for five Tour de France wins in the 1970s?
exp: Merckx is regarded as one of the greatest cyclists ever.

q: Which country won the UEFA Euro 2016?
exp: Portugal beat hosts France 1–0 in the final.

q: Which sport is played at Wimbledon?
exp: Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament, played on grass.
